metrics.name: Bank loans to nondepository financial institutions (H.8 NDFI line), all commercial banks, SA monthly
metrics.notes: The hidden bank exposure to nonbanks. ~$324B Jan-2015 -> ~$1.0T Jan-2024 -> ~$1.97T Apr-2026. Broad bucket: mortgage cos, REITs, BDCs, finance cos, broker-dealers, securitization vehicles AND private-credit/PE funds.

metrics.name: Bank loans to NDFIs, all commercial banks, weekly NSA
metrics.notes: Weekly H.8 print, not seasonally adjusted.

metrics.name: NDFI loans CAGR 2010-2024
metrics.notes: Fastest-growing bank loan category, ~3x the next-fastest. ~$1.14T Q1-2025 per FDIC/Call Report.

metrics.name: Bank committed exposures to private-credit obligors (narrow, Fed-measured)
metrics.notes: Specific bank->private-credit slice vs ~$1.6T Tier-1 capital of participating banks. Much smaller than the broad NDFI line.

metrics.name: Global private credit AUM
metrics.notes: Up from ~$300B (2010). Dimon cites $1.8T; FSB $1.5-2.0T end-2024; direct lending ~$800B (half). Forecast ~$2.64T by 2029.

metrics.name: CRE loans, all commercial banks (FRED), NSA monthly
metrics.notes: On-balance-sheet bank CRE; multifamily-heavy.

metrics.name: Delinquency rate on CRE loans (ex-farmland), banks
metrics.notes: Up from ~1.42% (2024-Q2). Bank book lags the office-heavy CMBS book.

metrics.name: CRE maturity wall 2025 (commercial mortgages maturing)
metrics.notes: MBA. +3% vs $929B that matured in 2024. ~24% of office loans come due in 2025.

metrics.name: CRE maturity wall 2026
metrics.notes: 17% of the $5.0T outstanding. S&P alt: $1.148T in 2026.

metrics.name: CRE maturity wall peak (2027)
metrics.notes: S&P: peaks 2027 at ~$1.26T ($1.257T); $950B in 2024. Principal frames total as ~$2T wall.

metrics.name: Maturing office loans underwater (debt > value)
metrics.notes: ~30% of maturing office loans on properties worth less than the debt (IMF GFSR).

metrics.name: Office CMBS delinquency rate (record)
metrics.notes: All-time record (Trepp), +63bps m/m, vs 11.08% Jun / 11.66% Aug 2025. >$1.7B office loans newly delinquent in Oct.

metrics.name: Overall CMBS delinquency rate
metrics.notes: Trepp, +23bps m/m.

metrics.name: Office value peak-to-trough decline (Green Street)
metrics.notes: Green Street CPPI office. Core all-property CPPI ~-24% from peak.

metrics.name: CBD office value peak-to-trough decline (MSCI)
metrics.notes: MSCI/RCA CPPI CBD office; older/subprime assets worse. Brackets requested -30% to -50% range at the deep end.

metrics.name: Office vacancy rate (record)
metrics.notes: Demand ~30% below pre-pandemic.

metrics.name: NYCB CRE concentration / total capital
metrics.notes: Trigger of Jan-2024 regional-bank scare. >300% is the FDIC/interagency concentration red flag.

metrics.name: Banks with CRE loans >300% of equity (<$100B assets)
metrics.notes: Per Fitch. Other flagged: Valley National ~479%, Washington Federal ~363%, Bank OZK ~355%.

metrics.name: AI/data-center private-credit pipeline (projected, next 2 years)
metrics.notes: Morgan Stanley projection, not realized. Ares cites ~$900B TAM for third-party DC investment.

metrics.name: Outstanding loans to AI-related companies
metrics.notes: From ~zero a few years ago. Includes GPU-backed loans.

metrics.name: Blackstone data-center holdings + pipeline
metrics.notes: Schwarzman: >$150B DCs globally + ~$160B prospective pipeline; 'largest investor in AI-related infrastructure.'

chain_edges.instrument: H.8 'loans to NDFIs' (warehouse, subscription/NAV, repo, term loans)
chain_edges.notes: Broadest top-of-chain bank exposure. ~6x growth since 2015. Bucket includes mortgage cos/REITs/BDCs/securitization plus private-credit & PE funds.

chain_edges.instrument: committed credit lines to private-credit obligors
chain_edges.notes: Specifically the PC slice inside NDFI; vs ~$1.6T Tier-1 capital.

chain_edges.from: Private-credit funds / asset managers (Blue Owl, Apollo, Blackstone, PIMCO, BlackRock, KKR)
chain_edges.notes: Meta Hyperion JV: ~$27B debt + ~$2.5B equity; Blue Owl 80%/Meta 20%; PIMCO ~$18B, BlackRock ~$3B. Largest private-credit deal ever. Some reports $29-30B.

chain_edges.to: Next-gen data centers / AI infrastructure (incl. Valor/xAI, Stream)
chain_edges.notes: >$40B deployed in 2025; $3.5B Valor/xAI; majority stake Stream Data Centers.
Sum of named facilities: CoreWeave $7.5B + Fluidstack $10B + Lambda $0.5B (+ Crusoe, unsized). Collateral = depreciating GPUs.
PROJECTION, not realized. Indicates scale of intended chain expansion.

chain_edges.instrument: 80/20 JV financing the Hyperion datacenter (~$29.5B; Blue Owl 80% / Meta 20%); off Meta's balance sheet (Morgan Stanley arranged)
bought the privately-placed Hyperion securities (A+, maturing 2049)
$8.5B GPU-backed delayed-draw financing (DDTL 4.0; first IG-rated GPU-backed financing) + earlier $2.3B (Magnetar/Blackstone)
~$12.5B GPU SPV (Apollo/Diameter/Valor) buys Nvidia chips and leases them to xAI 5yr (Colossus 2); off xAI's balance sheet; Nvidia ~$2B participation
~$15B Stargate Abilene project-finance JV (Blue Owl + Primary Digital) for OpenAI's Stargate datacenters

warnings.claim: AI value-chain 'circular financing'; nonbank/private-credit interconnectedness rising; ~$30B maturing office loans underwater
private credit's complex interlinkages with banks, leverage, valuation opacity, tech-sector concentration; 'banks are a critical node'
supervisory work on banks' interconnections with NBFIs
'one cockroach...probably more'; private credit 'worse than people think' at cycle turn; AI capex a 2026 risk
building tools to measure counterparty exposures to private credit (the chain is hard to see end-to-end)

unverifiable_notes: The NDFI ~$1.97T is a broad bucket; the datacenter-specific bank exposure inside it is NOT separately published (core measurement gap; OFR still building it).
$800B datacenter private-credit pipeline is a Morgan Stanley projection, not realized; ranges $800-900B by definition.
Private-credit AUM $1.5-2.0T varies by source/definition (direct lending vs broad private credit).
Anthropic ~$36B and Meta $27B-vs-$29-30B figures are reported/in-progress and may move.
'Circularity' (Nvidia->OpenAI->Oracle->neoclouds->Nvidia) is qualitatively flagged by IMF but not quantified in dollars by official sources.
